pr.title: Use AT_ADDRESS macro for fixed-address declarations
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Introduced a portable AT_ADDRESS(x) macro in include/dolphin/types.h and converted fixed-address declarations across Dolphin OS, DSP, GX, CARD, DVD, and serial/time code from MWCC-only address syntax such as ': 0xCC005000' to declarations using AT_ADDRESS. The PR body states the purpose directly: the macro expands differently for PERMUTER, __MWERKS__, and other compilers so fixed-address declarations remain usable with decomp-permuter and non-MWCC compilers.
what_changed: Added AT_ADDRESS(x) to include/dolphin/types.h with compiler-specific definitions: PERMUTER maps to '= FIXEDADDR(x)', __MWERKS__ maps to ': (x)', and other compilers expand to nothing.
Replaced many direct MWCC fixed-address declarations using colon syntax with AT_ADDRESS, for example '__DSPRegs[] : 0xCC005000' became 'extern volatile u16 __DSPRegs[] AT_ADDRESS(0xCC005000)'.
Removed several local '#ifndef M2CTX' guards around fixed-address declarations in headers and replaced them with a centralized macro decision.

Converted OS low-memory globals such as OS_CURRENT_CONTEXT, OS_CURRENT_FPU_CONTEXT, OS_THREAD_QUEUE, OS_CURRENT_THREAD, OS_INTR_OLD, OS_INTR_CUR, Mem_Size, Simulated_Mem, and OS_SYSTEM_TIME to AT_ADDRESS declarations.

Converted memory-mapped hardware register declarations such as __DSPRegs, __VIRegs, IO, WGPIPE, __DIRegs, OS_PI, __MEMRegs, __PIRegs, SIRegs, DAT_cc002000, and DAT_cc003000 to AT_ADDRESS declarations.
Some declarations were also made explicitly extern when converted, including several fixed-address hardware/global declarations in headers and source files.
smart_moves: Centralized compiler-specific fixed-address syntax behind one macro instead of scattering '#ifndef M2CTX' or MWCC-only colon-address declarations throughout the codebase.
Preserved MWCC matching behavior by defining AT_ADDRESS(x) as ': (x)' under __MWERKS__ rather than changing the underlying fixed-address mechanism for the target compiler.
Made decomp-permuter support explicit with the PERMUTER branch using FIXEDADDR(x), addressing the PR-stated need to let fixed-address declarations work during permutation.
Allowed non-MWCC compilers to parse these declarations by making AT_ADDRESS(x) empty outside PERMUTER and __MWERKS__ contexts.
Applied the pattern broadly across headers and source files in one mechanical refactor, reducing future inconsistency around fixed-address declaration style.
decomp_lessons: When MWCC-specific syntax is needed for matching, hide it behind a macro if the same declarations must also be consumed by tools or non-MWCC compilers.
Fixed-address hardware and low-memory symbols should use a single project-wide abstraction rather than per-file conditional compilation.
For declarations shared with decomp-permuter, provide a PERMUTER-specific expansion rather than forcing the permuter to parse MWCC colon-address syntax.
Use AT_ADDRESS for named symbols that represent memory-mapped registers or fixed low-memory addresses, especially in Dolphin SDK code.
A portability refactor can still be matching-safe when it preserves the exact MWCC syntax under __MWERKS__.
naming_conventions: Macro name introduced: AT_ADDRESS(x), used as a suffix on a declaration before the semicolon.
PERMUTER-specific fixed-address helper used by the macro: FIXEDADDR(x).

Existing fixed-address global naming was preserved, including uppercase SDK-style names such as OS_CURRENT_CONTEXT, OS_THREAD_QUEUE, OS_CURRENT_THREAD, BOOT_REGION_START, BOOT_REGION_END, OS_INTR_OLD, OS_INTR_CUR, OS_SYSTEM_TIME, and hardware register arrays like __DSPRegs, __DIRegs, __MEMRegs, __PIRegs, __VIRegs.
The PR did not introduce evidence of broader symbol renaming beyond replacing declaration syntax.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: AT_ADDRESS(x) preserves MWCC fixed-address placement syntax via ': (x)' when __MWERKS__ is defined.
For decomp-permuter, AT_ADDRESS(x) expands to '= FIXEDADDR(x)', avoiding MWCC-only syntax while still modeling a fixed address.
For other compilers, AT_ADDRESS(x) expands away, making declarations parseable even when fixed-address placement is not supported.
The refactor replaced prior M2CTX guards with compiler/tool-aware macro behavior, which is a reusable tactic for code that must build or be analyzed in multiple compiler modes.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The available review metadata shows one review, but no review body or actionable feedback was included in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add or modify AT_ADDRESS declarations for new fixed-address globals.
Search for remaining raw MWCC ': 0x' fixed-address declarations that should be converted to AT_ADDRESS.
Check whether non-MWCC build paths rely on AT_ADDRESS expanding to empty or need stronger extern declarations.
Search for uses of FIXEDADDR in permuter-related code to understand the full fixed-address modeling convention.
